deduplication_summary.note: TECH2 hypothesis IDs H003-H008 each appear twice in the input with different content. Removal/retention is per-instance as described in duplicate group descriptions. TECH2-H004 and TECH2-H008 each have one instance removed and one retained; TECH2-H007 has both instances retained.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: mintYieldFee function lacks access control, allowing anyone to mint accrued yield fee shares to any arbitrary recipient, stealing fees intended for _yieldFeeRecipient
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All eight threats describe the identical missing access control on Vault.mintYieldFee(). Same root cause (no onlyOwner/recipient check), same function, same exploit (call mintYieldFee with attacker address). ECON-H001 selected for CRITICAL severity and most complete attack economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: sponsor() function allows anyone to forcibly override any user's TWAB delegation to SPONSORSHIP_ADDRESS, revoking their prize eligibility
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe the same griefing vector: calling sponsor(1 wei, victimAddress) unconditionally delegates victim's balance to SPONSORSHIP_ADDRESS. Same root cause in Vault._sponsor → TwabController.sponsor. TECH2-H003 here refers to the 'Sponsor Function Can Forcefully Revoke' instance. TECH-H002 selected for most comprehensive multi-contract inventory refs and precise code analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Vault._deposit skips safeTransferFrom when vault holds idle underlying assets, allowing depositors to mint shares for free by consuming pre-existing vault balance
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe the same _deposit logic flaw where _assets <= _vaultAssets results in no token transfer but full share minting. Same root cause in Vault._deposit. ECON-H005 selected for CRITICAL severity and detailed attack economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Single canary tier claim sets _nextNumberOfTiers = largestTierClaimed + 2 before threshold checks, bypassing claimExpansionThreshold and forcing premature tier expansion
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All eight threats describe the identical logic error in PrizePool._computeNextNumberOfTiers where the initial assignment from largestTierClaimed+2 bypasses threshold gates. TECH2-H004 here refers to the 'Canary Tier Claims Bypass' instance. TECH-H004 selected for most precise technical explanation of why the threshold check cannot reduce the pre-set value.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Vault.liquidate() compares _amountOut (shares) to _vaultAssets (underlying assets) — unit mismatch prevents idle asset sweep into yield vault
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe the same unit mismatch in liquidate() where shares are compared to asset amounts. ECON-H012 combines this with the free deposit consequence but the root cause is the liquidate comparison. TECH2-H005 here refers to the 'Unit Mismatch in Liquidate' instance. ECON-H006 selected for HIGH severity and most complete economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Malicious winner-set VaultHooks (beforeClaimPrize/afterClaimPrize) can revert, causing entire batch claim transaction to fail and griefing claimers
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe the same DoS vector: user-controlled hooks called without try/catch in batch claim loop. ECON-H018 adds fee evasion angle but same root cause. TECH2-H006 here refers to the 'Malicious afterClaimPrize Hook' instance. ECON-H004 selected for most comprehensive economics and victim impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: closeDraw casts uint256 contributions to uint96, silently truncating values exceeding ~79.2 billion tokens and permanently locking excess in the PrizePool
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five threats describe the same uint96 truncation in PrizePool.closeDraw → _nextDraw. TECH2-H008 here refers to the 'Silent Truncation' instance. ECON-H019 selected for HIGH severity and detailed analysis of permanently locked tokens.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PrizePool.contributePrizeTokens is permissionless and allows attributing unaccounted tokens to any vault address, enabling contribution credit theft
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four threats describe the same permissionless vault attribution via delta-balance pattern in contributePrizeTokens. Same function, same exploit (attribute others' tokens to attacker's vault). TECH-H007 selected for most comprehensive technical analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TwabController mint/burn/transfer are permissionless with msg.sender as vault key, allowing fake vault namespaces and potential TWAB manipulation if integration boundaries fail
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four threats describe the same TwabController design where external mint/burn/transfer use msg.sender as vault key without access control. TECH2-H003 here refers to the 'Permissionless mint/burn/transfer in TwabController' instance. TECH-H012 selected for most comprehensive analysis of integration risk and downstream impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PrizePool.claimPrize uses msg.sender as vault without validation, allowing any contract with controlled TwabController state and contributions to claim prizes as a fake vault
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same lack of vault authentication in PrizePool.claimPrize, enabling fake vault contracts to participate in prize distribution. TECH-H013 selected for more detailed technical analysis and higher severity.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: UD34x4 truncation in _consumeLiquidity loses up to 1e14 wei per claim, accumulating phantom tier liquidity that drains the reserve over time
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four threats describe the same UD34x4 precision loss in _consumeLiquidity → fromUD60x18toUD34x4. Same code defect, same accumulation mechanism, same reserve drain impact. ECON-H017 selected for HIGH severity and comprehensive analysis including DrawManager withdrawal angle.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TieredLiquidityDistributor view functions (getTierPrizeSize, getTierRemainingLiquidity) missing tier bounds validation, returning default values for out-of-range tiers
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same missing tier bounds check in TieredLiquidityDistributor external view functions. TECH2-H005 here refers to the 'tier index misuse in view methods' instance. TECH-H015 selected for more detailed investigation rationale.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PrizePool._openDrawEndsAt drift/skip logic when draws are missed causes draw ID gaps, contribution accounting inconsistencies, and potential loss of contributions for skipped draws
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the same _openDrawEndsAt catch-up behavior when draws are missed, where lastClosedDrawId only increments by 1 despite multiple periods passing. TECH2-H006 here refers to the '_openDrawEndsAt time catch-up' instance. ECON2-H021 selected for HIGH severity and most comprehensive analysis of contribution loss impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Vault exchange rate permanently ratchets down after yield vault loss, causing recovered principal to be classified as yield and liquidated away from depositors
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same _currentExchangeRate capping mechanism that permanently depresses the rate after a loss, treating recovery as liquidatable yield. Same root cause in exchange rate computation. ECON-H015 selected for CRITICAL severity.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Vault portion calculation uses _lastClosedDrawId + 1 as endDrawId, including open draw contributions in closed draw prize determination, enabling manipulation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same off-by-one in _computeVaultTierDetails where vault portion includes open draw contributions. Same root cause, same manipulation vector. ECON2-H008 selected for higher severity (HIGH) and more detailed proof of signal.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TWAB timestamp computation underflows for high-tier prizes during early protocol life, blocking prize claims for tiers with large draw durations
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the same uint32 arithmetic underflow in _getVaultUserBalanceAndTotalSupplyTwab when drawDuration * drawPeriodSeconds exceeds _endTimestamp for early draws. ECON2-H013 selected for most detailed analysis including specific draw count thresholds per tier.
